reason: Modifying the mount options for the /boot/efi partition alters the system's boot filesystem. An incorrect fstab entry can prevent the EFI partition from mounting, leading to an unbootable system. Policy classifies partitioning/filesystem/bootloader changes as too dangerous for automated remediation.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; potential boot failure; inability to mount EFI partition; system may become unbootable
